mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next month when policy is still moving lower. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating, because rate cuts then reflect genuine easing rather than late-cycle stress.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative more often than positive during months when FEDFUNDS is falling, or if the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL inflation is stable-to-decelerating.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE marks a labor-market break that reduces earnings expectations and raises recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal activates only when unemployment moves materially higher, not during flat labor conditions.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations generate positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 40% of cases, or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional baseline after the unemployment jump.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates stronger household demand and payroll income, which supports revenues and earnings revisions for the S&P 500. This interaction should predict positive returns over the next month only when both consumption and employment are improving together.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 20-day SPX returns do not concentrate in months where both RSAFS and PAYEMS are rising, or if either series alone explains the same effect without the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in INDPRO signals weaker current industrial activity and margin pressure, so equities should underperform over the next week when production momentum turns down. The mechanism is a near-term growth scare rather than a long-horizon recession call.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls for 2 consecutive monthly prints
failure_condition: Reject if the average 5-day forward return after consecutive INDPRO declines is not negative, or if the sign flips positive in the majority of such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in HOUST reflects improving residential demand and broader cyclical momentum, which should lift equity prices over the next quarter through better growth expectations and higher risk appetite. The effect is strongest when housing starts are recovering from a prior trough.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not above baseline after three-month housing recovery streaks, or if the signal is absent when GDP growth is already slowing sharply.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above a restrictive threshold and CPIAUCSL inflation is still elevated, the market discounts further tightening or prolonged real-rate pressure, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails when inflation is already falling fast because then high policy rates are more likely near a peak.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation remains above its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show negative average 20-day returns, or if the effect persists even when CPIAUCSL is clearly decelerating.

mechanism_narrative: A rising PAYEMS trend without a matching increase in UNRATE indicates broad labor absorption and wage-income support, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next week. The signal is not meant to fire when payroll gains coexist with rising unemployment, which would indicate labor-supply churn rather than genuine strength.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises month-over-month and UNRATE does not rise
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day forward SPX returns are not positive after payroll gains with flat or lower unemployment, or if payroll gains with rising UNRATE produce the same positive response.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in RSAFS after prior strength signals weakening retail demand and softer earnings momentum, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when the drop occurs alongside slower industrial activity, confirming that demand is broadly rolling over.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines month-over-month after at least 2 prior positive months
failure_condition: Reject if post-peak RSAFS declines do not lead to below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal remains negative even when INDPRO is accelerating rather than weakening.
